Package component.mover

Class FlipFlop

java.lang.Object
component.mover.Mover
component.mover.FlipFlop
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tippable, GridIndexable
public class FlipFlop extends Mover
The FlipFlop class is a stateful Mover that toggles between two opposing directions.

Every time a card successfully interacts with this tile, the internal state flips, causing the next card to be sent in the 180-degree opposite direction.

  • Method Details

    • isActive

      public boolean isActive()
      Checks the current toggle state.
      Returns:
      true if the primary direction is active.

    • setActive

      public void setActive(boolean active)
      Manually sets the toggle state.
      Parameters:
      active - The new state to set.

    • getDirectionStateless

      public Direction getDirectionStateless()
      Determines the current movement direction without triggering a toggle.
      Specified by:
      getDirectionStateless in class Mover
      Returns:
      The currently active Direction.

    • getDirection

      public Direction getDirection()
      Retrieves the movement direction and toggles the state for the next card.

      The flip only occurs if a card is actually present on the tile, preventing the state from oscillating during empty logic ticks.

      Overrides:
      getDirection in class Mover
      Returns:
      The Direction allowed for the current card.

    • getValidOutputDirections

      public Direction[] getValidOutputDirections()
      Lists all possible directions this mover can point to.
      Specified by:
      getValidOutputDirections in class Mover
      Returns:
      An array containing the primary rotation and its opposite.

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Resets the mover to its primary starting direction.
      Overrides:
      reset in class Mover

    • getTooltip

      public Tooltip getTooltip()
      Generates a dynamic tooltip showing the current routing direction.
      Specified by:
      getTooltip in interface Tippable
      Overrides:
      getTooltip in class Mover
      Returns:
      A Tooltip explaining the alternating behavior.
